regimeSub: Risk-on reflation with sticky 4.2% CPI and a hawkish Warsh Fed that dropped its cutting bias as oil shock fades on Iran deal.
themes: US-Iran ceasefire caps Brent near $73 -> oil-driven CPI spike loses fuel, but core stickiness keeps Fed hawkish
Warsh Fed strips cutting bias with CPI at 3-yr high -> September HIKE risk back on the table, front-end repricing
AI/energy capex supercycle drives equities to records -> Dow >52k, SPX 7440, tech leadership broadening
Gold at $4000 despite risk-on -> structural debasement/fiscal-dominance bid persists alongside soft DXY at 101

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy 3.63% vs CPI 4.2% = negative real front end; Fed reluctant to ease, hike risk rising.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +31bp (4.07/4.38), modestly positive; bear-steepening risk if Warsh signals hikes on sticky inflation.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Tight; VIX 17.65 and record equities signal no credit stress, complacency into hawkish Fed.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 17.65, low post-ceasefire; geopolitical premium drained, leaves little cushion for a Fed hawkish surprise.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $73.52, back below pre-war as US-Iran deal halts attacks; supply-surge narrative overshooting downside.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4002, holding records despite risk-on/soft oil; pure fiscal-dominance and debasement hedge bid.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1547 weak KRW despite KOSPI 8647 surge; JPYKRW 9.54, carry/semis flows masking soft USD backdrop.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: AI capex supercycle leadership; KOSPI/Nikkei record highs confirm chips driving global risk appetite.

headline: Equities print record highs on a US-Iran ceasefire and AI capex boom, but a hawkish Warsh Fed and 4.2% CPI keep a September hike live, not cuts.
